groups.blurb: Camelids closely related to llamas, raised here for their fiber — the source of the socks, gloves, and yarn in our shop.
Scottish cattle with long horns and shaggy coats, bred to handle harsh weather — the herd you'll meet at Coffee with the Cows.
A cross between the Scottish Belted Galloway and the small, docile Jersey dairy breed.
A modern American miniature breed descended from West African dwarf goats.
groups.unnamedNote: plus ~7 male kids and ~7 yearlings not yet individually named

groups.blurb: A hairy, rotund New Zealand breed — docile enough to live entirely on pasture grass.
groups.unnamedNote: plus a herd of ~10 unnamed pigs

groups.blurb: Laverne and Shirley, our resident mules.
Penelope (mini horse, notorious attitude) and Murphy (mini donkey, textbook donkey temperament) round out the barnyard.
A laying flock of chickens, Indian Runner ducks on garden pest-control duty, Bourbon Red turkeys, and a new 2026 silkie breeding line.
groups.unnamedNote: laying flock (count varies) · Indian Runner ducks · Bourbon Red turkeys (mostly reserved) · silkie breeding stock (not yet for sale)
